mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.13% as of 2024-09-01 suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines fail to exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) over two consecutive months indicates weakening consumer demand and aggregate spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth forecasts.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 710773.0M (vintage) vs 697039.0M (revised) as of 2024-08-01 shows downward revision
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite sustained retail sales decline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with stable or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals improving supply-side productivity and real output growth without cost-push pressures, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite favorable supply-inflation combination, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over two consecutive months signals labor market weakness that reduces household income and consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159105.0K (vintage) vs 157912.0K (revised) as of 2024-09-01 shows significant downward revision
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite sustained payroll decline, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below the prior 3-month average signals weakening residential investment demand and forward construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice cyclical growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite sharp housing starts decline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP (GDPC1) combined with rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust aggregate demand and labor income growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates and equity risk premiums compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises > 2% annualized AND PAYEMS rises > 150K month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite strong GDP and payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the central bank maintains restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite restrictive monetary conditions, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
